SCROLL
Profile

An engineer who never left the lab, and a professor who never left the circuit.

Anto Manuel is an Assistant Professor, engineering academic, and researcher with more than a decade of experience spanning teaching, research, academic administration, and product development. He is currently pursuing a Ph.D. at IIT Goa, developing biosensors using MEMS and nanomaterials.

His work moves fluidly between the classroom and the cleanroom — building curricula and laboratories on one side, and glassy-carbon acetone sensors and carry-select adders on the other. He has led accreditation drives (NAAC, NBA), coordinated final-year research for hundreds of students, and authored peer-reviewed journal papers recognised with multiple international Best Paper Awards.

Alongside the research, he has spent seven years as an NSS Programme Officer, coordinating the construction of homes for underprivileged families and organising societal-benefit camps — proof that his idea of "engineering impact" extends well past the PCB.

Ph.D.
IIT Goa, Biosensors — ongoing
M.Tech
VLSI & Embedded Systems — 8.86 CGPA, Univ. 3rd Rank
B.E.
Electronics & Communication, Anna University
Journey

From production floor to the professor's chair

A single trace connecting a decade of roles — production engineering, R&D, teaching, and academic leadership.

JUNE 2025 — PRESENT
Assistant Professor · HoD · Deputy Controller of Examinations
Rajadhani Institute of Engineering and Technology, Thiruvananthapuram
  • Leads the Department of Biomedical Engineering — faculty, curriculum, labs, research culture, and accreditation standards.
  • Serves as Deputy Controller of Examinations in the Autonomous Cell, overseeing evaluation, results, and revaluation with strict confidentiality.
MARCH 2016 — MAY 2025
Assistant Professor
St. Joseph's College of Engineering and Technology, Palai
Nine years across analog/digital electronics, embedded systems, and MEMS — designing on STM32, PIC, ATmega and AT89 families, managing the Product Design Lab, and coordinating the M.Tech VLSI & Embedded Systems programme.
  • Conceived and built the college's Heritage Museum, commended by senior leadership.
  • Led NAAC Criterion 4 and NBA accreditation documentation and execution.
  • Guided final-year student projects for 4+ years; ran admissions for 8 years.
  • NSS Programme Officer for 7 years — 11 homes built, seven social-benefit camps.
DEC 2015 — MAR 2016
Research and Development Engineer
Simstream Pvt Ltd, Kerala
Translated client needs into embedded system designs — market analysis, conceptualisation, prototyping, and cost-reduction, tested to industrial standards.
AUG 2012 — JUNE 2013
Production Engineer
IPA Pvt Ltd, Bengaluru
PLC ladder logic for industrial automation, process optimisation, equipment calibration, and production scheduling.
2021 — CONTINUING
Ph.D., Biosensors, MEMS & IoT
Indian Institute of Technology Goa
Glassy carbon, photolithography, and Carbon-MEMS sensor fabrication — four publications, two book chapters, and three Best Paper Awards along the way.
2013 — 2015
M.Tech, VLSI & Embedded Systems
St. Joseph's College of Engineering and Technology · Mahatma Gandhi University
8.86 CGPA, University Third Rank Holder. Thesis: an embedded, Android-controlled vehicle security system, deployed in a real car.
2008 — 2012
B.E., Electronics & Communication Engineering
Maha Barathi Engineering College · Anna University, Chennai
7.92 CGPA. Main project: a low-power, area-efficient carry-select adder in VLSI design.
Selected Research

Sixteen papers. One recurring question: can a sensor see what we can't?

Publications trace a single thread — glassy-carbon and Carbon-MEMS sensors for acetone and disease biomarkers — alongside forays into AI-driven public health and disaster prediction.

2026
Microelectronic Devices — Book Chapter 2
"Microelectronics: Simulations, Modeling and Applications," Wiley & Scrivener
2025
Carbon-MEMS derived glassy carbon sensor for acetone sensing
Microsystem Technologies, Vol. 31, 3579–3592
2026
Nanocomposite Coated Carbon-MEMS-Derived Glassy Carbon Electrode for Acetone Sensing
6th Int'l Conf. on Micro/Nanoelectronics, NIT Silchar
Best Paper
2025
Sensor response of a glassy-carbon IDE biosensor for acetone, modelled in COMSOL Multiphysics
6th ICCC, Thiruvananthapuram
Best Paper
2025
Glassy Carbon based Acetone Detector using Carbon-MEMS Technology
5th Int'l Conf. on Micro/Nanoelectronics, NIT Silchar
Best Paper
2024
Elderly Fall Detection System Using mm-Wave Radar Sensor
ICAECT 2024, Bhilai
2023
IoT-enabled biosensors for cancer and disease detection — Book Chapter 4
Internet of Things in Biomedical Sciences, IOP Publishing
2023
AI-integrated IoT smart mask for disease prediction & SOS alerting
IConSCEPT 2023, NIT Puducherry
Best Paper

Building a lab, a curriculum, or a sensor? Let's talk.
